src/interest_calculator.py.note: 100% line coverage but 1/2 tests fail due to known bugs (simple interest formula instead of compound).

known_bugs_in_code: calculate_compound_interest() uses Simple Interest formula (P*(1+r*t)) instead of Compound Interest (P*(1+r/n)^(nt))
calculate_compound_interest() uses float instead of decimal.Decimal
format_currency() uses float rounding, causing precision errors

step_1_denoising.method: Attempted to reproduce each issue on the main branch by inspecting source files and running the code.

issues.title: Refactor Database Connection Pool
issues.stale_reason: No database connection pool code exists anywhere in the repository. The repo contains only a single-file interest calculator.

issues.title: Integration with Legacy Auth Provider
issues.stale_reason: No SOAP API integration or auth provider code exists in the repository. The referenced legacy library handling has no footprint in the codebase.

issues.title: [ARCHITECTURAL] Migrate Entire Monorepo to Pydantic V2
issues.stale_reason: Issue describes 40+ modules using standard dictionaries, but the repo has only 1 source module (interest_calculator.py) with no dictionary-based data models or Pydantic usage.

issues.title: Floating Point Precision Drift in reporting.py
issues.reproduction: reporting.py does not exist, BUT the underlying bug IS reproducible in src/interest_calculator.py — format_currency() uses float rounding. Example: format_currency(100.000000004) returns '$100.0' instead of '$100.00'.

scoring.business_impact_reason: Financial precision directly affects revenue calculations; code handles monetary values.
scoring.fixability_reason: Bug is in a local logic file (src/interest_calculator.py). Fix: replace float with decimal.Decimal in format_currency().
scoring.blast_radius_reason: interest_calculator.py is imported by only 1 module (test_calculator.py), well below the >5 threshold.
scoring.regression_risk_reason: Module has 100% line coverage (4/4 statements covered).

issues.bucket_reason: High Impact + High Fixability → suitable for parallel Devin execution.

issues.title: Implement Leap Year Logic for Annual Interest
issues.reproduction: Confirmed: current formula uses P*(1+r*t) with no day-based logic. Leap year handling is entirely absent. Valid enhancement request.

scoring.business_impact_reason: Financial accuracy for annual interest is a revenue-critical concern; incorrect day counts produce wrong payouts.
scoring.fixability_reason: Requires new calculation logic (day-count convention) beyond a simple one-line fix, but stays within the local logic file.
scoring.blast_radius_reason: interest_calculator.py is imported by only 1 module.
scoring.regression_risk_reason: Module has 100% line coverage; however, new logic will need new tests.

issues.bucket_reason: High Impact + Medium Fixability → suitable for parallel Devin execution.

issues.title: [SECURITY] Sanitize User Input in api_handler.py
issues.stale_reason: api_handler.py does not exist in the repository. No API handler or input sanitization code is present.

issues.title: [CRITICAL] Negative Principal Handling in Math Logic
issues.reproduction: Reproduced: calculate_compound_interest(-1000, 0.05, 2) returns -1100.0 without any validation error. Negative principal is silently accepted.

scoring.business_impact_reason: Negative principal produces nonsensical negative interest payouts — direct revenue and financial integrity impact.
scoring.fixability_reason: Fix is a simple validation guard in local logic file (src/interest_calculator.py).
scoring.blast_radius_reason: interest_calculator.py is imported by only 1 module.

issues.bucket_reason: High Impact + High Fixability → suitable for parallel Devin execution.

Stale.description: Not reproducible on main branch per Step 1 Vanish protocol.

recommendations: Close issues #26, #27, #30, #33, #34, #35 as Stale — they reference files or infrastructure that does not exist in this repository.
Prioritize #29 (Negative Principal) as the highest-severity active bug — it silently produces incorrect financial outputs.
Address #32 (Float Precision) alongside any formula fix, since switching to decimal.Decimal resolves both the formula and precision issues.
#31 (Leap Year) is an enhancement that should be scoped after the core formula is corrected.
#28 (README) can be auto-fixed with minimal risk.
